NKJV.1: To the Chief Musician. With stringed instruments. A Psalm of David. Hear me when I call, O God of my righteousness! You have relieved me in my distress; Have mercy on me, and hear my prayer.
NKJV.2: How long, O you sons of men, Will you turn my glory to shame? How long will you love worthlessness And seek falsehood? Selah
NKJV.3: But know that the LORD has set apart for Himself him who is godly; The LORD will hear when I call to Him.
NKJV.4: Be angry, and do not sin. Meditate within your heart on your bed, and be still. Selah
NKJV.5: Offer the sacrifices of righteousness, And put your trust in the LORD.
NKJV.6: There are many who say, “Who will show us any good?” LORD, lift up the light of Your countenance upon us.
NKJV.7: You have put gladness in my heart, More than in the season that their grain and wine increased.
NKJV.8: I will both lie down in peace, and sleep;  For You alone, O LORD, make me dwell in safety.

ESV.1: Answer me when I call, O God of my righteousness! You have given me relief when I was in distress. Be gracious to me and hear my prayer!
ESV.2: O men, how long shall my honor be turned into shame? How long will you love vain words and seek after lies? Selah
ESV.3: But know that the LORD has set apart the godly for himself; the LORD hears when I call to him.
ESV.4: Be angry, and do not sin; ponder in your own hearts on your beds, and be silent. Selah
ESV.5: Offer right sacrifices, and put your trust in the LORD.
ESV.6: There are many who say, “Who will show us some good? Lift up the light of your face upon us, O LORD!”
ESV.7: You have put more joy in my heart than they have when their grain and wine abound.
ESV.8: In peace I will both lie down and sleep; for you alone, O LORD, make me dwell in safety.

NASB.1: For the choir director; on stringed instruments. A Psalm of David. Answer me when I call, O God of my righteousness! You have relieved me in my distress; Be gracious to me and hear my prayer.
NASB.2: O sons of men, how long will my honor become a reproach? [How long] will you love what is worthless and aim at deception? Selah.
NASB.3: But know that the LORD has set apart the godly man for Himself; The LORD hears when I call to Him.
NASB.4: Tremble, and do not sin; Meditate in your heart upon your bed, and be still. Selah.
NASB.5: Offer the sacrifices of righteousness, And trust in the LORD.
NASB.6: Many are saying, 'Who will show us [any] good?' Lift up the light of Your countenance upon us, O LORD!
NASB.7: You have put gladness in my heart, More than when their grain and new wine abound.
NASB.8: In peace I will both lie down and sleep, For You alone, O LORD, make me to dwell in safety.

NLT.1:
Answer me when I call to you,
O God who declares me innocent.
Free me from my troubles.
Have mercy on me and hear my prayer.
NLT.2:
How long will you people ruin my reputation?
How long will you make groundless accusations?
How long will you continue your lies? Interlude
NLT.3:
You can be sure of this:
The LORD set apart the godly for himself.
The LORD will answer when I call to him.
NLT.4:
Don’t sin by letting anger control you.
Think about it overnight and remain silent. Interlude
NLT.5:
Offer sacrifices in the right spirit,
and trust the LORD.
NLT.6:
Many people say, “Who will show us better times?”
Let your face smile on us, LORD.
NLT.7:
You have given me greater joy
than those who have abundant harvests of grain and new wine.
NLT.8:
In peace I will lie down and sleep,
for you alone, O LORD, will keep me safe.

WEB.1: For the Chief Musician; on stringed instruments. A Psalm by David. Answer me when I call, God of my righteousness. Give me relief from my distress. Have mercy on me, and hear my prayer.
WEB.2: You sons of men, how long shall my glory be turned into dishonor? Will you love vanity, and seek after falsehood? Selah.
WEB.3: But know that Yahweh has set apart for himself him who is godly: Yahweh will hear when I call to him.
WEB.4: Stand in awe, and don’t sin. Search your own heart on your bed, and be still. Selah.
WEB.5: Offer the sacrifices of righteousness. Put your trust in Yahweh.
WEB.6: Many say, “Who will show us any good?” Yahweh, let the light of your face shine on us.
WEB.7: You have put gladness in my heart, more than when their grain and their new wine are increased.
WEB.8: In peace I will both lay myself down and sleep, for you, Yahweh alone, make me live in safety.

AMP.1:
To the Chief Musician; on stringed instruments. A Psalm of David. Answer me when I call, O God of my righteousness!
You have freed me when I was hemmed in and relieved me when I was in distress;
Be gracious to me and hear [and respond to] my prayer.
AMP.2:
O sons of men, how long will my honor and glory be [turned into] shame?
How long will you [my enemies] love worthless (vain, futile) things and seek deception and lies? Selah.
AMP.3:
But know that the LORD has set apart for Himself [and dealt wonderfully with] the godly man [the one of honorable character and moral courage—the one who does right].
The LORD hears and responds when I call to Him.
AMP.4:
Tremble [with anger or fear], and do not sin;
Meditate in your heart upon your bed and be still [reflect on your sin and repent of your rebellion]. Selah.
AMP.5:
Offer righteous sacrifices;
Trust [confidently] in the LORD.
AMP.6:
Many are saying, "Oh, that we might see some good!"
Lift up the light of Your face upon us, O LORD.
AMP.7:
You have put joy in my heart,
More than [others know] when their wheat and new wine have yielded abundantly.
AMP.8:
In peace [and with a tranquil heart] I will both lie down and sleep,
For You alone, O LORD, make me dwell in safety and confident trust.

MSG.1:
A David psalm
When I call, give me answers. God, take my side!
Once, in a tight place, you gave me room;
Now I'm in trouble again: grace me! hear me!
MSG.2:
You rabble—how long do I put up with your scorn?
How long will you lust after lies?
How long will you live crazed by illusion?
MSG.3:
Look at this: look
Who got picked by God!
He listens the split second I call to him.
MSG.4:
Complain if you must, but don't lash out.
Keep your mouth shut, and let your heart do the talking.
MSG.5: Build your case before God and wait for his verdict.
MSG.6:
Why is everyone hungry for more? "More, more," they say.
"More, more."
I have God's more-than-enough,
MSG.7:
More joy in one ordinary day
Than they get in all their shopping sprees.
MSG.8:
At day's end I'm ready for sound sleep,
For you, God, have put my life back together.

NIV.1:
Psalm 4
For the director of music. With stringed instruments. A psalm of David.
Answer me when I call to you,
O my righteous God.
Give me relief from my distress;
be merciful to me and hear my prayer.
NIV.2:
How long, O men, will you turn my glory into shame?
How long will you love delusions and seek false gods? Selah
NIV.3:
Know that the Lord has set apart the godly for himself;
the Lord will hear when I call to him.
NIV.4:
In your anger do not sin;
when you are on your beds,
search your hearts and be silent. Selah
NIV.5:
Offer right sacrifices
and trust in the Lord.
NIV.6:
Many are asking, “Who can show us any good?”
Let the light of your face shine upon us, O Lord.
NIV.7:
You have filled my heart with greater joy
than when their grain and new wine abound.
NIV.8:
I will lie down and sleep in peace,
for you alone, O Lord,
make me dwell in safety.

NRSVCE.1:
Answer me when I call, O God of my right!
You gave me room when I was in distress.
Be gracious to me, and hear my prayer.
NRSVCE.2:
How long, you people, shall my honor suffer shame?
How long will you love vain words, and seek after lies? Selah
NRSVCE.3:
But know that the LORD has set apart the faithful for himself;
the LORD hears when I call to him.
NRSVCE.4:
When you are disturbed, do not sin;
ponder it on your beds, and be silent. Selah
NRSVCE.5:
Offer right sacrifices,
and put your trust in the LORD.
NRSVCE.6:
There are many who say, “O that we might see some good!
Let the light of your face shine on us, O LORD!”
NRSVCE.7:
You have put gladness in my heart
more than when their grain and wine abound.
NRSVCE.8:
I will both lie down and sleep in peace;
for you alone, O LORD, make me lie down in safety.

NET.1: When I call out, answer me, O God who vindicates me! Though I am hemmed in, you will lead me into a wide, open place. Have mercy on me and respond to my prayer!
NET.2: You men, how long will you try to turn my honor into shame? How long will you love what is worthless and search for what is deceptive? (Selah)
NET.3: Realize that the LORD shows the godly special favor; the LORD responds when I cry out to him.
NET.4: Tremble with fear and do not sin! Meditate as you lie in bed, and repent of your ways! (Selah)
NET.5: Offer the prescribed sacrifices and trust in the LORD!
NET.6: Many say, “Who can show us anything good?” Smile upon us, LORD!
NET.7: You make me happier than those who have abundant grain and wine.
NET.8: I will lie down and sleep peacefully, for you, LORD, make me safe and secure.
